We are given the 4×4 matrix:

$$
A = \begin{bmatrix}
-3 & -4 & -2 & 3 \\
0 & 4 & -4 & -2 \\
3 & -3 & 4 & -2 \\
4 & 2 & -2 & -1
\end{bmatrix}
$$

We are to find the **determinant** of this matrix.

---

### Step 1: Use the cofactor expansion along the first row

The determinant of a 4×4 matrix can be computed using cofactor expansion along the first row:

$$
\det(A) = -3 \cdot \det\begin{bmatrix}
4 & -4 & -2 \\
-3 & 4 & -2 \\
2 & -2 & -1
\end{bmatrix}
- (-4) \cdot \det\begin{bmatrix}
0 & -4 & -2 \\
3 & 4 & -2 \\
4 & -2 & -1
\end{bmatrix}
+ (-2) \cdot \det\begin{bmatrix}
0 & 4 & -2 \\
3 & -3 & -2 \\
4 & 2 & -1
\end{bmatrix}
- 3 \cdot \det\begin{bmatrix}
0 & 4 & -4 \\
3 & -3 & 4 \\
4 & 2 & -2
\end{bmatrix}
$$

---

-3 & 4 & -2 \\
2 & -2 & -1
\end{bmatrix}
$$

= 4 \cdot \det\begin{bmatrix}
4 & -2 \\
-2 & -1
\end{bmatrix}
- (-4) \cdot \det\begin{bmatrix}
-3 & -2 \\
2 & -1
\end{bmatrix}
+ (-2) \cdot \det\begin{bmatrix}
-3 & 4 \\
2 & -2
\end{bmatrix}
$$
